ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Sprite Zero Sugar; mapped to the broader class 'carbonated beverage' (FOODON_03301589) as the closest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN0KL covers diet/low-calorie carbonated soft drinks; used as the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor for Sprite Zero Sugar. No brand-specific FoodEx2 code exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical concept maps to a branded zero-sugar carbonated soft drink at this specificity level.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 320 ml can of Sprite Zero Sugar, as sold at Wingstop Singapore and other fast-food outlets, equivalent to approximately 320 g by weight.

nutrient_source.title: Sprite Zero Sugar — USDA Branded Food (FDC ID 2346498 / Sprite Zero Sugar 355ml reference, scaled to per-100g basis)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA Branded Food entry for Sprite Zero Sugar (355 ml can, The Coca-Cola Company) used as the primary nutrient reference. Values are expressed per 100 g / 100 ml. The Singapore 320 ml can is the same Sprite Zero Sugar formulation; per-100g values are directly applicable. Coca-Cola product label data for Singapore market corroborates the zero-calorie, zero-sugar, zero-fat, zero-protein profile. Sodium content confirmed from USDA Branded Food entry (~28 mg per 240 ml serving, scaled to ~12 mg per 100 ml).

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of artificially sweetened beverages and diet sodas — University of Sydney GI Database reference; diet carbonated soft drinks
gi_source.source_match_notes: Sprite Zero Sugar contains no glycemic carbohydrates (0 g sugar, 0 g total carbohydrate per 100 ml). The University of Sydney GI Database lists diet/sugar-free carbonated soft drinks as having a GI of effectively 0 (no glycemic response). Per the Veritas zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel) and gi_category is 'low'. Glycemic load per 320 ml serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g is 0.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~38 mg per 320 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: Sprite Zero Sugar contributes approximately 38 mg of sodium per 320 ml can, which is well within safe limits for hypertensive individuals; however, patients should account for total daily sodium from all dietary sources and note that regular consumption of artificially sweetened beverages has been associated in some observational studies with adverse cardiometabolic outcomes.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero glycemic carbohydrates; artificially sweetened with aspartame and acesulfame potassium
type2_diabetes.guidance: Sprite Zero Sugar contains no sugar or glycemic carbohydrates and will not raise blood glucose acutely, making it a lower-risk beverage choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes compared to regular sugar-sweetened sodas; nonetheless, clinical guidelines recommend limiting artificially sweetened beverages and preferring water, as long-term effects on insulin sensitivity and gut microbiome remain under investigation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero cholesterol, zero sugar; low sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Sprite Zero Sugar is free of fat, cholesterol, and sugar, posing minimal direct cardiovascular risk per serving; however, individuals with cardiovascular disease should be aware that habitual consumption of artificially sweetened beverages has been associated in prospective cohort studies with increased cardiovascular event risk, and water or unsweetened beverages remain the preferred choice per major cardiovascular guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sprite Zero Sugar (320 ml can) is a zero-calorie, sugar-free carbonated soft drink produced by The Coca-Cola Company and sold widely across Singapore, including at fast-food chains such as Wingstop. It is a lemon-lime flavoured sparkling beverage sweetened with aspartame and acesulfame potassium instead of sucrose or high-fructose corn syrup, delivering the characteristic crisp, citrus-forward taste of Sprite without any caloric contribution. The drink contains no protein, no fat, no carbohydrates, and no sugar, making it nutritionally inert from a macronutrient perspective. Its sodium content is low at approximately 12 mg per 100 ml (approximately 38 mg per 320 ml can). Because it contains aspartame, it carries a phenylketonuria (PKU) warning for individuals with phenylketonuria. Sprite Zero Sugar is suitable for vegetarians, vegans, and those following halal dietary requirements. It is classified as an ultra-processed beverage. From a glycemic standpoint, it produces no measurable blood glucose response and is appropriate for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes who wish to consume a flavoured carbonated beverage. Clinically, while it avoids the sugar load of regular Sprite, health professionals note that long-term habitual consumption of artificially sweetened beverages may be associated with adverse metabolic and cardiovascular outcomes in some population studies, and water remains the preferred hydration choice. In Singapore, it is commonly ordered as a meal accompaniment at Western fast-food and fried chicken restaurants.

Is Sprite Zero Sugar suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Sprite Zero Sugar is a globally standardised branded product manufactured by The Coca-Cola Company. Nutrient values (zero calories, zero macronutrients, low sodium) are confirmed by USDA Branded Food entries for Sprite Zero Sugar and are consistent with the Coca-Cola Singapore product label. The per-100g profile is directly applicable to the 320 ml Singapore can. No estimation of macronutrients was required.
notes: Zero-macro justification: energy_kcal is confirmed zero — this is a zero-calorie artificially sweetened carbonated beverage by product formulation (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods). protein_g contains no protein — confirmed zero by product formulation. fat_g contains no fat — confirmed zero by product formulation. carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero — this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; sweetness is provided entirely by aspartame and acesulfame potassium (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central). sugar_g is confirmed zero by formulation. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is a carbonated beverage with no plant-derived fibre. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero by formulation. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are confirmed zero by formulation. Sentinel value (-1) fields: potassium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food entry for Sprite Zero Sugar. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Glycemic index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per Veritas zero-calorie beverage glycemic convention. Glycemic load per 320 ml serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g is 0. PKU warning: contains phenylalanine (from aspartame). Input cuisine tag 'Fried Chicken' reflects the restaurant context (Wingstop) rather than the food's own cuisine classification; corrected to 'Global / Multinational' for the beverage. Multilingual names provided for English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi in line with Singapore market context. Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, and Vietnamese local_names are null as this is a globally branded product not requiring those language fields for this record's cuisine classification.
